componentDescription: TextArea is a multi-line text input for longer free-form content — descriptions, comments, messages, and notes. Use TextArea when the expected input is more than one sentence. For short single-line text, use TextInput.

usageExamples.title: Comment or note field
usageExamples.description: TextArea for entering a comment with character limit.
usageExamples.code:
<TextArea
  label="Comment"
  placeholder="Add a comment..."
  value={comment}
  onChange={setComment}
  rows={4}
  maxLength={500}
  hintText="Be specific and constructive"
/>

usageExamples.title: Product description with error
usageExamples.code:
<TextArea
  label="Description"
  sublabel="Required"
  placeholder="Describe the product in detail..."
  value={description}
  onChange={setDescription}
  rows={6}
  maxLength={2000}
  error={!description.trim() && submitted}
  errorMessage="Description is required"
  size={TextInputSize.MEDIUM}
/>

usageExamples.title: Support message with no resize
usageExamples.code:
<TextArea
  label="Message"
  placeholder="Describe your issue in detail"
  value={message}
  onChange={setMessage}
  rows={8}
  maxLength={1000}
  resize="none"
/>

value.llmContext: Required. Controlled textarea value. Manage with useState initialized to empty string or pre-populated content.

onChange.llmContext: Required. Called with new string value on every keystroke.
onChange.propDescription: Required. Called with new string on input change

rows.llmContext: Initial visible height of the textarea in text rows. Use 3-4 for short descriptions and notes. Use 6-8 for longer content like messages and essays. The user can resize beyond this if resize is not 'none'.

maxLength.llmContext: Maximum character limit. Shows a character counter below the textarea (e.g., '234/500'). Setting maxLength also enforces the limit in the input. Always display the limit so users can plan their input.
maxLength.propDescription: Maximum character count — shows counter when set

resize.llmContext: 'none' prevents user resizing — use for fixed-layout forms where textarea size affects surrounding elements. 'vertical' (default) lets users resize height only. 'both' allows both axes.

error.llmContext: Set to true for validation errors (empty required field, content policy violation, etc.). Pair with errorMessage.
error.propDescription: Shows error styling on the textarea
